rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Western Allied Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 9, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Western Allied Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiff¿s objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 9, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Intervenors Hartford Accident and Indemnity Co., et al., on behalf of defendant Alta Mechanical¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and their alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages is off calendar. Intervenors failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs¿ claims against Alta Mechanical. Further, even if Intervenors had demonstrated that the sophisticated use defense were applicable, such defense applies only to the duty to warn and is not a complete bar to plaintiffs¿ claims. Intervenors also failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs¿ cause of action for premises owner/contractor liability must fail even if plaintiffs cannot demonstrate that Alta Mechanical retained control over Mr. Peterson¿s work/jobsites. Plaintiffs dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 9, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Monterey Mechanical Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ strict liability cause of action is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Peterson and, more specifically, that any harm to Mr. Peterson attributable to defendant was foreseeable. By failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to special discovery designed to elicit all evidence and information plaintiffs have to support their contention that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Peterson, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs¿ claims against it. Further, even if defendant had demonstrated that the sophisticated use defense were applicable, such defense applies only to the duty to warn and is not a complete bar to plaintiffs¿ claims. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD SIMMONS VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 9, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff¿s claims are barred by the statute of limitations. (C.C.P. 340.2.) Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether his claim is barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiff suffered a disability as defined by C.C.P. 340.2 when he had to stop working on and sell his avocado grove. More than one year passed from the time plaintiff knew, or in the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, that his disability was caused by exposure to asbestos and the filing of his complaint. The Court further finds that plaintiff Harold Simmons¿ declaration conflicts with his sworn deposition testimony and should be excluded. (D¿Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 22; Preach v. Monter Rainbow (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1441, 1451; Visueta v. General Motors Corp. (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 1609.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
